name: Riaño, Pandetrave, and Panderrueda Loop
name_original: Sierra de Riaño – circular por Pandetrave y el Valle de Sajambre
description: This difficult 44.1-mile touring cycling route in Sierra de Riaño offers challenging terrain and stunning views of the Picos de Europa.
region_intro: No traffic touring cycling routes around Embalse de Riaño navigate a landscape characterized by a large reservoir encircled by limestone mountains and extensive Atlantic forests. The region, often referred to as the "Leonese fjords," is part of the Regional Park of Riaño and Mampodre, known for its biodiversity. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, from reservoir shores to mountain passes, offering diverse natural environments. This area provides a network of routes suitable for touring cyclists seeking car-free paths through dramatic scenery. Best no traffic touring cycling routes around Embalse de Riaño The most popular no traffic touring cycling route is Riaño Swing – Valcayo Viewpoint loop from Riaño, a 6.9 miles (11.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 2 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding mountains. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Valcayo Viewpoint – Riaño Swing loop from Riaño, a difficult 21.7 miles (34.9 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and panoramic vistas of the "Leonese fjords." Local touring cyclists also love the Zaco River Valley – Lois Historic Village loop from Liegos, a 13.3 miles (21.4 km) trail leading through river valleys and past traditional villages, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Touring cycling around Embalse de Riaño is defined by limestone mountains, a vast reservoir perimeter, and rich Atlantic forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Embalse de Riaño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 16 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Embalse de Riaño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic touring cycling routes are available around Embalse de Riaño?
A: There are 9 dedicated no-traffic touring cycling routes around Embalse de Riaño featured in this guide. These routes are designed to offer serene cycling experiences away from vehicular traffic, allowing you to fully immerse yourself in the stunning natural landscape.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of no-traffic touring cycling routes in Embalse de Riaño?
A: The no-traffic touring cycling routes in Embalse de Riaño range from moderate to difficult. You'll find 3 moderate routes and 6 difficult routes, often featuring significant elevation gains as they navigate the mountainous terrain surrounding the reservoir. Cyclists should be prepared for varied surfaces and climbs.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the no-traffic routes in Embalse de Riaño?
A: The no-traffic touring cycling routes around Embalse de Riaño are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 out of 5 stars from 16 ratings. Reviewers often praise the breathtaking panoramic views, the tranquility of the routes, and the unique natural features of the 'Leonese fjords' area.

Q: Are there any circular no-traffic touring cycling routes around Embalse de Riaño?
A: Yes, several circular no-traffic touring cycling routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaño Swing – Valcayo Viewpoint loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 11 km ride, while the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcayo Viewpoint – Riaño Swing loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 35 km with significant elevation.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the no-traffic touring cycling routes?
A: The terrain on these routes is diverse, reflecting the region's mountainous character. While some sections may be paved, many routes incorporate unpaved or gravel segments, especially as they wind through the Atlantic forests of oak and beech. Expect varied surfaces and elevation changes, typical of the Cantabrian Mountains.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the no-traffic touring cycling routes?
A: The routes offer stunning views of the Embalse de Riaño, often referred to as the 'Leonese fjords', and the surrounding limestone mountains. You can encounter unique natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6811232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted Pine Forest with Wooden Masks&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also pass by panoramic viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6308896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Riaño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive vistas of the water and the Picos de Europa.

Q: Are there any family-friendly no-traffic touring cycling routes in Embalse de Riaño?
A: While many routes feature moderate to difficult ratings due to elevation, the 11 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaño Swing – Valcayo Viewpoint loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that might be suitable for families with some cycling experience. It offers a taste of the region's beauty without the most strenuous climbs.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a no-traffic touring cycling route?
A: Parking is generally available in the main towns and villages that serve as starting points for these routes, such as Riaño, Liegos, Prioro, or La Revoltona. These locations often have designated parking areas for visitors exploring the Embalse de Riaño and the Regional Park of Riaño and Mampodre.

Q: What is the best time of year to cycle the no-traffic routes around Embalse de Riaño?
A: The best time to cycle is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and temperatures are pleasant for cycling. The lush Atlantic forests are vibrant in spring and summer, and the autumn colors are spectacular. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out, as mountain weather can be unpredictable.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest along these routes?
A: Yes, you can discover cultural heritage along some routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaco River Valley – Lois Historic Village loop from Liegos&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the historic village of Lois. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hórreo of Salio - Riaño&lt;/a&gt;, a traditional rural monument, is a notable highlight in the area, offering a glimpse into local history.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on these no-traffic routes?
A: The Embalse de Riaño area, being part of the Regional Park of Riaño and Mampodre and a gateway to the Picos de Europa National Park, is rich in biodiversity. While cycling, you might spot various bird species, chamois, or roe deer. The region is also home to brown bears and grouse, though sightings of these shyer animals are less common.
tips: Impressive views of Picos de Europa and the Valley of Santa Marina de Valdeón. There are interpretive panels and a car park from where you can start your route.

Throughout this stretch up or down, depending on your direction, between Posada de Valdeón and the viewpoint in the Panderrueda pass, you will have incredible views of the Picos de Europa and its valleys and green slopes.

A stretch that you will surely enjoy with a big smile.

Riaño, known as the "new Riaño," rises next to the reservoir that bears its name, surrounded by spectacular mountains in the heart of the León mountains. It is a picturesque and strategic location for exploring the surrounding peaks and valleys, as well as the Picos de Europa.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a difficult touring bicycle route, requiring very good fitness. It covers approximately 70 km with over 1,300 meters of elevation gain, taking nearly 5 hours for experienced riders. Some sections may be unpaved, loose, and steep, potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of mostly paved roads and some unpaved segments. You'll traverse mountainous regions with spectacular alpine views, deep green valleys, and impressive limestone peaks. Expect diverse landscapes, including ancient beech and oak forests, and some sections described as 'loose and steep'.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the exact starting point for parking isn't specified, Riaño is a common access point for the region. You can look for parking options in or around Riaño to begin your circular journey.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The autumn months are particularly beautiful due to the vibrant colors of the beech and oak forests. Generally, spring through early autumn would offer the most favorable weather conditions for cycling in this mountainous region, avoiding winter's challenging conditions.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints and highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers exceptional views of the Picos de Europa and the Valley of Santa Marina de Valdeón. Key highlights include the Pandetrave Viewpoint, the View of the Picos de Europa from the road to Puerto de Panderrueda, and the View of Riaño and the Reservoir, often called the 'Leonese fjords'.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant portions of the Camino Vadiniense (38%) and the Transcantábrica (17%). Smaller segments also align with routes like [CIMA LE15] Pandetrave, [CIMA LE10] Panderruedas * Caín, and El Anillo de Picos (3 Macizos).

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Riaño – circular by Pandetrave and the Valle de Sajambre route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Picos de Europa National Park generally allows dogs, specific regulations may apply to cycling routes and protected areas. It's always best to check local signage or contact the park authorities directly regarding leash rules and access for dogs on this particular cycling trail, especially given its challenging nature and potential for wildlife encounters.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: This route passes through the Parque Regional de la Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre. Generally, there are no specific entrance fees for accessing regional parks in Spain for activities like cycling. However, it's always wise to be aware of any specific regulations or protected zones within the park that might have restrictions or require permits for certain activities. No specific cycling permits are typically required for this type of route.

faqs.question: What kind of flora and fauna might I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers opportunities to observe diverse flora and fauna within the protected natural parks. You'll cycle through ancient beech (hayedos) and oak forests. The region is known for its biodiversity, so keep an eye out for local wildlife, though specific sightings are never guaranteed.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route, especially in Valle de Sajambre?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through charming villages such as Oseja de Sajambre and others within the Valdeón Valley. These villages are good places to find cafes, restaurants, or small shops to refuel and rest during your ride.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and mountainous terrain, you should pack essentials like plenty of water, high-energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), appropriate cycling gear for varying weather conditions, a first-aid kit, and navigation tools. Due to some unpaved sections, sturdy tires are also recommended.
